audit_purpose: Audit 82 no-idempotency-key surfaces (sister to m12ji). Different bug class: no gate at all vs gate-after-side-effect.
methodology: Regex-heuristic scanner + manual spot-check per m12ji pattern. Scanner classifies into 4 verdicts; manual review of each NEEDS_KEY for false-positive triage.

false_positive_reclass_as_read_only.evidence: Line 432: --apply explicitly rejected with fail_json 3 apply_not_supported_read_only_bridge
false_positive_reclass_as_read_only.scanner_miss_reason: scanner detected --apply token but didn't detect the rc=3 refusal pattern

false_positive_reclass_as_read_only.evidence: Documented mutates_only_with: --apply but apply-path is 'create or reuse a repo-local rework bead on BLOCK_CLOSE' — reuse-mode is idempotent. Also: line 509 'if [ MODE != apply ]' suggests rare apply path.
false_positive_reclass_as_read_only.scanner_miss_reason: scanner counted mutation patterns but didn't notice the 'reuse' semantics in apply path

false_positive_reclass_as_read_only.evidence: End-to-end smoke test; --apply runs the test (no external mutation, only validates).
false_positive_reclass_as_read_only.scanner_miss_reason: scanner counted internal test-fixture writes as mutations

false_positive_reclass_as_idempotent.evidence: Line 78: writes to mktemp .XXXXXX file then os.replace → atomic-replace pattern is structurally idempotent
false_positive_reclass_as_idempotent.scanner_miss_reason: scanner didn't detect the mktemp + os.replace chain as idempotent (would require multi-line context)

false_positive_reclass_as_idempotent.evidence: Line 191-192: apply path is write_readonly_marker (idempotent — writing a marker file twice yields the same marker)
false_positive_reclass_as_idempotent.scanner_miss_reason: function name 'write_readonly_marker' didn't match scanner's idempotent-hint patterns

false_positive_reclass_as_idempotent.evidence: Line 143: apply_receipt(receipt) writes a receipt — receipt files overwrite-replace idempotently
false_positive_reclass_as_idempotent.scanner_miss_reason: apply_receipt name didn't match scanner's idempotent-hint patterns

false_positive_reclass_as_idempotent.evidence: Python probe writes JSON conformance receipt with write_json helper that does atomic-replace
false_positive_reclass_as_idempotent.scanner_miss_reason: write_json wrapper name not in idempotent patterns

false_positive_reclass_as_idempotent.evidence: plist writes to stable launchd path; re-run writes the same plist content (plist generation is pure-function)
false_positive_reclass_as_idempotent.scanner_miss_reason: plistlib.dump matched mutation but plist-to-stable-path pattern not captured

false_positive_reclass_as_idempotent.evidence: MODE=apply: mkdir -p + content sync (write_if_changed-equivalent via cp + diff check)
false_positive_reclass_as_idempotent.scanner_miss_reason: did not detect the diff-then-cp pattern

false_positive_reclass_as_idempotent.evidence: Daily corpus diff + notify-on-storage; idempotent under same input. Re-run produces same notification (notify dedup is a separate concern).
Daily diff output to canonical path; re-run produces same output.
--apply == DRY_RUN=0; surface emits ntm rebalance receipt; receipt is read-only output (ntm rebalance itself is the mutator and has its own gating)

tier_1_genuine_bug_class.mutation_kind: ntm send ping to other panes (external state, non-idempotent)
tier_1_genuine_bug_class.rationale: Each --apply invocation sends fresh pings; re-running double-pings the worker. --idempotency-key would prevent double-ping on operator re-run.

tier_1_genuine_bug_class.mutation_kind: cross-fleet doctrine sync (writes to AGENTS.md, validation schemas, doctrine docs, launchd templates, .claude/settings.json across all flywheel-installed repos)
tier_1_genuine_bug_class.rationale: Backup-before-write mitigates accidental clobber but doesn't prevent double-apply from clobbering a backup. Largest blast radius of any audited surface. --idempotency-key + content-hash dedupe would prevent re-run from re-rewriting unchanged files.
git commit + push to remote is non-idempotent at the remote-state level. Re-run after an interrupted install could double-commit.

tier_1_genuine_bug_class.mutation_kind: regenerate Jeff source-repo copies (rm + clone + integrate)
tier_1_genuine_bug_class.rationale: Backup-before-write helps but the operation itself is destructive-then-replace. Concurrent re-runs could collide.

tier_1_genuine_bug_class.mutation_kind: br set priority=0 on detected hub blockers (beads JSONL mutation)
tier_1_genuine_bug_class.rationale: br set on a bead is non-idempotent if called twice (audit-trail double-row). --idempotency-key + br action-id dedupe would prevent double-escalation.
Specific-purpose capture; lower frequency but still non-idempotent at audit-trail level.
Task execution path is not naturally idempotent; re-run could re-execute callbacks. --idempotency-key would dedupe.
